opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running an agent at the OS level creates interesting security boundaries—you need to carefully scope what filesystem and network operations are permitted, or you're trading privacy for convenience. I've found that daemon-based agents with proper IPC actually handle resource contention better than true kernel extensions, since they can be more easily constrained and restarted without destabilizing the system.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building AI agents with multi-provider routing, tool orchestration, and OS integration patterns.
